response.justification: Strong technical fundamentals and demonstrated leadership capability, but has a significant gap in the core requirement of 8 years of C++ programming (resume shows only ~2 years from 2014-2015). Career trajectory is primarily product and web engineering rather than specialized cloud infrastructure and compute platform work. Could potentially succeed with willingness to deepen C++ and cloud architecture expertise, but represents a notable ramp-up from current foundation.
strengths: Demonstrated strong software craftsmanship culture across multiple roles with emphasis on TDD, CI/CD, and testing practices
Proven technical leadership at staff level with evidence of mentoring through workshops, courses, open-source contributions, and team leadership
Extensive experience with large-scale distributed systems (Dunnhumby millions of requests/second) and diverse technical domains showing adaptability
concerns: Critical gap in required C++ expertise: only ~2 years of C++ experience versus 8-year minimum requirement; primary languages are TypeScript, JavaScript, and D

Limited specialized experience building cloud compute platforms or large-scale infrastructure—career focused primarily on product engineering, web services, and data integration rather than compute/infrastructure platforms
No evidence of formal advanced degree or dedicated technical leadership role specifically mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers as preferred qualifications
